title: Order Dockerfile layers from least to most volatile
description: Place dependency-install steps before copying application source so a code change does not bust the dependency cache layer on every build.
body: Use when writing or optimizing a Dockerfile's build steps. Do not COPY the whole source tree before installing dependencies, and avoid putting frequently-changing files high in the layer stack. Actions: copy only the manifest/lockfile first, run the dependency install as its own layer, then copy the rest of the source; inspect the build to confirm the install layer is reused on a code-only change. Validation signals include a rebuild after a source edit showing the dependency layer 'CACHED' and a measurably shorter build time. Risk boundary: if a build reinstalls all dependencies on every code change, the layer order is wrong; fix ordering rather than disabling the cache, and avoid cache tricks that mask a stale lockfile.
